prompt: I just have my home garage — bandsaw, drill press, hand tools, an orbital sander, a router, and a few clamps. No CNC, no laser. I want to build a 3-tier hexagonal lazy susan from solid walnut. Bottom tier is 16 inches across (point-to-point), each tier steps down by 2 inches. Just give me a shop-ready Tier 1 plan, I don't need anything fancy.

assertions.text: Tier 1 file set complete: design.md, bom.csv, cut-list.csv, op-sequence.md, safety-notes.md all present
No Tier 2/3 over-delivery (no README, drawings/, deck, sourcing.csv, validation.csv, risks.md, assembly-manual.md)
op-sequence.md uses ONLY the user's stated tools — no CNC, laser, mill, jointer, planer, tablesaw mentioned as required ops
op-sequence.md does mention bandsaw plus at least one of drill press / sander / router
design.md contains hexagon math (apothem, side length, or across-flats)
